title: GLOBAL_COAP_RULE and GLOBAL_OSCORE_RULE allow any global address but spec may require specific prefixes
description:
File: python/src/lichen/schc/headers.py:62 and rules.py:246-248

_is_global() checks:
  return addr >> 125 == 0b001  # 2000::/3

This matches any address in the 2000::/3 range, which includes:
- 2001::/32 (Teredo, deprecated)
- 2001:db8::/32 (documentation)
- 2002::/16 (6to4, deprecated)
- 2001:10::/28 (ORCHID)
- Plus legitimate global unicast

RFC 8724 SCHC typically operates in specific deployment contexts where the address prefixes are known. Using the broad 2000::/3 match may allow packets with deprecated or reserved prefixes to use the global rules when they should not.

Additionally, GLOBAL_COAP_RULE uses CDA.VALUE_SENT for the full 128-bit address (no compression), while LINK_LOCAL_COAP_RULE uses MSB(64)/LSB to compress the IID. This asymmetry means global addresses get no compression benefit.

Fix: Consider documenting the address scope assumptions, or adding rule variants for specific deployment prefixes.

Category: PROTOCOL
close_reason: Updated _is_global() and documentation to reflect current 02xx::/7 primary global addresses per spec/04-network.md and 06-security.md. Tightened matching, updated comments, test, lint, rules.py. Matches bead description.
